content.problemStatement: Write a C program to turn the string "CIVVIX" into a Palindrome. To do this, take the last character of the string and make changes to the string as needed. Finally, print the resulting palindrome.
constraints: Length of each dummy string is at most 100 characters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains an arbitrary string which should be ignored by the program.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the constant palindrome string "CIVVIC" on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case; regardless of the provided string, the program outputs the palindrome "CIVVIC".
Two test cases are processed. For each, the constant palindrome "CIVVIC" is printed on its own line.
Even empty lines are accepted as dummy input; the program still prints the required palindrome three times.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
10
s1
s2
s3
s4
s5
s6
s7
s8
s9
s10
hidden.expectedOutput:
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC

solution.approach: The problem does not require any processing of the input strings. The only task is to read the number of test cases T, discard the following T lines, and print the fixed palindrome "CIVVIC" T times. This can be done with a simple loop. No data structures or complex algorithms are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.
